The accounts ledger (balance paid, A-062) and client ledger (picked up March 2021, C-017) both indicate collection. Only the unidentified third-hand correction in the parts ledger claims otherwise. Two sources versus one unverified source.
Clive Maughton
20%, stating it was a revised agreement
15%, with Aldric's note saying to keep at 15% until Maughton provides written confirmation

The 15% rate from the client ledger. Keates recommends this because the client ledger note references the dispute explicitly and appears to be the later, more considered position, while the accounts note reads as an initial concession that Aldric later reconsidered.
Under the recommended 15% rate, the estate owes Maughton £2,720 from the sale proceeds. If the 20% rate applied, it would be £2,560, meaning the estate already overpaid by £160.
No. The client ledger note (C-024) explicitly states there is no written record of the changed agreement.

The document does not confirm whether it has been shipped. The instruction was given January 14, and Aldric died February 9. The executor lists this as an open question.

Yes. The marginal note states Vickers accepted the condition as suitable for decorative use, not precision.
The document does not confirm pickup. The January 2025 inventory lists it as 'COMPLETE, awaiting pickup.' The executor lists this as unresolved.

Dearborn regulator: SOLD. Whitford bracket clock: IN SHOP (per the P-094 correction). Hartley marine chronometer: IN STORAGE, return to client pending. Ansonia shelf clock: COMPLETE, awaiting pickup.

The Whitford bracket clock (disputed whether collected or in shop), the Hartley marine chronometer (instruction to ship given but not confirmed completed), the Ansonia shelf clock (complete but not confirmed picked up), the Maughton commission dispute (15% vs 20%), and the balance spring disposition.
